steps.title: Track the central shift
steps.content: The passage begins with Dorothy Allen but quickly broadens to the rich history of Black journalism in the United States. It then explains that the National Association of Black Journalists preserves and honors that history.

steps.title: Avoid narrow or unsupported claims
steps.content: Choice A isolates one founding detail instead of the main idea. Choice B expands beyond the United States, which the passage does not do. Choice C invents a claim that Allen's achievements are overlooked abroad. The answer is D.
